mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household spending, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a period of stable inflation signals robust capacity utilization that supports corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3pp monthly, indicating that supply growth is being offset by margin compression from input cost inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects aggregate economic expansion, which improves corporate profit expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week following each data release.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations following positive GDP growth surprises, or the rolling 1-year correlation between GDP growth and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.5% sustain high borrowing costs that suppress equity valuations and corporate investment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for > 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, or the forward return distribution shows no statistical difference between periods above and below the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and low unemployment rates indicate a tight labor market that eventually forces wage inflation, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in the specified direction, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for CPIAUCSL changes in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signal renewed consumer confidence and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL shows accelerating inflation, or the predictive power vanishes in a walk-forward test spanning multiple business cycles.
